description: EVAL-01 golden (EN, economic). Golden reference + a high-quality candidate that should score >= 0.80 on completeness / accuracy-vs-reference / bilingual-parity. Synthetic data only — no real dossier names or classified content.

input.descEn: A mid-size trading partner; bilateral trade volume grew 12% year on year.
input.descAr: شريك تجاري متوسط الحجم؛ نما حجم التبادل التجاري الثنائي بنسبة 12% على أساس سنوي.

golden.summary: Astrabia is a mid-size economic partner with bilateral trade up 12% year on year, driven by energy and agricultural exports. A free-trade memorandum is under negotiation, with the third round scheduled for next quarter.

sections.title: Trade Posture
sections.content: Two-way trade reached 4.2 billion last year, a 12% increase. Energy accounts for 58% of imports from Astrabia; agricultural goods make up 31% of exports to it.

sections.title: Open Negotiations
sections.content: A free-trade memorandum is in its second round. The main open item is tariff schedules on processed foods; a third round is set for next quarter.

sections.title: Outlook
sections.content: Trade momentum is positive but exposed to commodity-price swings. Concluding the memorandum would lock in tariff certainty for both sides.

candidate.summary: Astrabia is a mid-size trade partner. Bilateral trade rose 12% over the past year, led by energy imports and agricultural exports. A free-trade memorandum is being negotiated and reaches its third round next quarter.

sections.title: Trade Posture
sections.content: Bilateral trade was 4.2 billion last year, up 12%. Energy is the largest import category from Astrabia at roughly 58%, while agricultural products lead exports to it at about 31%.

sections.title: Open Negotiations
sections.content: The free-trade memorandum is in round two. Tariff schedules on processed foods remain the principal sticking point, with round three planned for next quarter.

sections.title: Outlook
sections.content: The trade trend is favorable but sensitive to commodity prices. Finalizing the memorandum would give both parties tariff predictability.
